steps.title: Identify the relationship between the two sentences
steps.content:
The first sentence describes the rocket failure. The second sentence describes the CEO's public response to that failure. The blank must signal that the CEO's response came after the event — a time-sequence relationship.
  • Choice A — Afterward,: A time-sequence marker meaning "following that event," which correctly places the CEO's response after the failure. ✓
  • Choice B — Additionally,: Adds information to a list; it does not establish that one event followed another in time. ✗
  • Choice C — Indeed,: Confirms or emphasizes the previous statement; the CEO's response is not a confirmation of the failure. ✗
  • Choice D — Similarly,: Signals that something is like something else; the CEO's response is not analogous to the rocket failure. ✗

steps.title: Confirm the answer
steps.content: Only "Afterward" establishes that the CEO's public acknowledgment came in response to the failure as a subsequent event. That confirms A is correct.
